title: अर्धनारीश्वर स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ अथ श्रीअर्धनारीश्वरस्तोत्रम् ॥

चाम्पेयगौरार्धशरीरकायै
कर्पूरगौरार्धशरीरकाय।
धम्मिल्लकायै च जटाधराय
नमः शिवायै च नमः शिवाय॥१॥

कस्तूरिकाकुंकुमचर्चितायै
चितारजःपुञ्जविचर्चिताय।
कृतस्मरायै विकृतस्मराय
नमः शिवायै च नमः शिवाय॥२॥

झणत्क्वणत्कंकणनूपुरायै
पादाब्जराजत्फणिनूपुराय।
हेमांगदायै भुजगांगदाय
नमः शिवायै च नमः शिवाय॥३॥

विशालनीलोत्पललोचनायै
विकासिपंकेरुहलोचनाय।
समेक्षणायै विषमेक्षणाय
नमः शिवायै च नमः शिवाय॥४॥

मन्दारमालाकलिताललकायै
कपालमालांकितकन्धराय।
दिव्याम्बरायै च दिगम्बराय
नमः शिवायै च नमः शिवाय॥५॥

अम्भोधरश्यामलकुन्तलायै
तटित्प्रभाताम्रजटाधराय।
निरीश्वरायै निखिलेश्वराय
नमः शिवायै च नमः शिवाय॥६॥

प्रपञ्चसृष्ट्युन्मुखलास्यकायै
समस्तसंहारकताण्डवाय।
जगज्जनन्यै जगदेकपित्रे
नमः शिवायै च नमः शिवाय॥७॥

प्रदीप्तरत्नोज्ज्वलकुण्डलायै
स्फुरन्महापन्नगभूषणाय।
शिवान्वितायै च शिवान्विताय
नमः शिवायै च नमः शिवाय॥८॥

एतत्पठेदष्टकमिष्टदं यो
भक्त्या स मान्यो भुवि दीर्घजीवी।
प्राप्नोति सौभाग्यमनन्तकालं
भूयात्सदा तस्य समस्तसिद्धिः॥

॥ इति श्रीमच्छंकराचार्यविरचितम् अर्धनारीश्वरस्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Atha Shri Ardhanareeshwara Stotram ||

Chaampeyagauraardha-Shareerakaayai
Karpooragauraardha-Shareerkaaya.
Dhammillakaayai Cha Jataadharaaya
Namah Shivaayai Cha Namah Shivaaya.||1||

Kastoorikaa-Kumkuma-Charchitaayai
Chitaarajahpunja-Vicharchitaaya.
Kritasmaraayai Vikritasmaraaya
Namah Shivaayai Cha Namah Shivaaya.||2||

Jhanatkvanat-Kankana-Noopuraayai
Paadabjaraajat-Phani-Noopuraaya.
Hemaangadaayai Bhujagaangadaaya
Namah Shivaayai Cha Namah Shivaaya.||3||

Vishaala-Neelotpala-Lochanaayai
Vikaasi-Pankeruha-Lochanaaya.
Samekshanaayai Vishamekshanaaya
Namah Shivaayai Cha Namah Shivaaya.||4||

Mandaaramaalaa-Kalita-Alakaayai
Kapaalamaalaankita-Kandharaaya.
Divyaambaraayai Cha Digambaraaya
Namah Shivaayai Cha Namah Shivaaya.||5||

Ambhodhara-Shyaamala-Kuntalaayai
Tatitprabhaataamra-Jataadharaaya.
Nireeshvaraayai Nikhileshvaraaya
Namah Shivaayai Cha Namah Shivaaya.||6||

Prapancha-Srishtyunmukha-Laasyakaayai
Samasta-Samhaarakataa-Ndavaaya.
Jagajjananyai Jagadeka-Pitre
Namah Shivaayai Cha Namah Shivaaya.||7||

Pradeeptaratnojjvala-Kundalaayai
Sphuranmahaa-Pannaga-Bhooshanaaya.
Shivaanvitaayai Cha Shivaanvitaaya
Namah Shivaayai Cha Namah Shivaaya.||8||

Etat-Pathed-Ashtakam-Ishtadam Yo
Bhaktyaa Sa Maanyo Bhuvi Deerghajeevee.
Praapnoti Saubhaagyam-Anantakaalam
Bhooyaat Sadaa Tasya Samasta-Siddhih.

|| Iti Shrimach-Chankaracharyavirachitam Ardhanareeshwara Stotram Sampoornam ||
hindiMeaning:
१. (बायाँ अर्ध — देवी) चम्पक पुष्प जैसी गौर आधे शरीर वाली को, (दायाँ अर्ध — शिव) कर्पूर जैसे गौर आधे शरीर वाले को; केशों की धम्मिल्ल (चोटी) वाली को और जटाधारी को — शिवा (पार्वती) को नमस्कार, शिव को नमस्कार।

२. कस्तूरी-कुंकुम से अर्चित (देवी) को, चिताभस्म से विचर्चित (शिव) को; कामदेव को सृजने वाली (देवी) को, कामदेव को विकृत (भस्म) करने वाले (शिव) को — शिवा को और शिव को नमस्कार।

३. झनझन बजते कंकण-नूपुर वाली (देवी) को, चरणकमल पर चमकते सर्प-नूपुर वाले (शिव) को; स्वर्ण अंगद (बाजूबन्द) वाली (देवी) को, सर्प के अंगद वाले (शिव) को — नमस्कार।

४. विशाल नील कमल जैसे नेत्रों वाली (देवी) को, विकसित कमल जैसे नेत्रों वाले (शिव) को; सम (दो) नेत्रों वाली (देवी) को, विषम (तीन) नेत्रों वाले (शिव) को — नमस्कार।

५. मन्दार माला से सजी अलकों वाली (देवी) को, कपालमाला से अंकित कन्धे वाले (शिव) को; दिव्य वस्त्रधारी (देवी) को, दिगम्बर (शिव) को — नमस्कार।

६. मेघ जैसे श्यामल केशों वाली (देवी) को, विद्युत जैसी ताम्र जटाओं वाले (शिव) को; निरीश्वरा (ऐसी जिसका कोई ऊपर न हो — देवी) को, निखिलेश्वर (समस्त के ईश्वर — शिव) को — नमस्कार।

७. प्रपंच (सृष्टि) रचने के लिए लास्य (कोमल नृत्य) करने वाली (देवी) को, समस्त संहार का ताण्डव करने वाले (शिव) को; जगज्जननी (देवी) को, जगत के एकमात्र पिता (शिव) को — नमस्कार।

८. प्रदीप्त रत्नों के उज्ज्वल कुण्डल वाली (देवी) को, स्फुरित महासर्प आभूषण वाले (शिव) को; शिव से युक्त (देवी) को, शिवा (पार्वती) से युक्त (शिव) को — नमस्कार।

जो भक्तिपूर्वक इस इष्ट-प्रद अष्टक का पाठ करता है, वह भूमि पर मान्य, दीर्घजीवी होता है, अनन्तकाल तक सौभाग्य प्राप्त करता है और उसे सदा समस्त सिद्धियाँ मिलती हैं।

viniyog.shloka: अस्य श्रीअर्धनारीश्वरस्तोत्रमन्त्रस्य श्री आदि शंकराचार्य ऋषिः। उपजाति छन्दः। श्री अर्धनारीश्वर (शिव-शक्ति) देवता। ॐ नमः शिवाय बीजम्। शिव-शक्ति ऐक्य शक्तिः। अर्धनारीश्वर प्रसाद कीलकम्। श्रीअर्धनारीश्वरप्रीत्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः॥

benefits: Worships Shiva and Shakti as one inseparable form
Beautifully contrasts feminine and masculine aspects in each verse
Invokes marital harmony and union of complementary energies
Invokes the blessing of long life, fame, and all-round prosperity (saubhagya)
Invokes all siddhis (spiritual accomplishments) to the devoted reciter
Reveals the non-dual philosophy of Shiva-Shakti unity
Composed by Adi Shankaracharya — a masterpiece of devotional poetry
description: Among the most cherished prayers to Lord Shiva, Ardhanareeshwara Stotram (अर्धनारीश्वर स्तोत्रम्) stands as a devotional hymn of praise. This sacred composition is sourced from Composed by Adi Shankaracharya and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to grant marital harmony and union of complementary energies as well as bestow long life, fame, and all-round prosperity (Saubhagya). Additionally, it is known to reveal the non-dual philosophy of Shiva-Shakti unity. For best results, devotees chant this on Monday (Somvar) and Friday (Shukravar) and during Maha Shivaratri, Shravan month, and Navratri. Comprising 8 verses, it can be completed in approximately 5 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Ardhanareeshwara Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Ardhanareeshwara Stotram by Shankaracharya in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Eight verses praising the half-male half-female form of Shiva-Parvati.
relatedSearches: ardhanarishvara stotram meaning verse by verse

seoFaqs.question: Who composed the Ardhanareeshwara Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ardhanareeshwara Stotram is universally attributed to Adi Shankaracharya (c. 8th century CE), the Advaita acharya credited with consolidating Smarta worship across India. It appears in the standard Stotra-Ratnavali compilations of his works alongside the Soundarya Lahari, Shivanandalahari and Annapurna Stotram. The hymn is an example of Shankara's distinctive ability to use shleshalankara (paired masculine and feminine Sanskrit gender forms in the same line) to philosophically demonstrate the inseparability of Shiva and Shakti. While later commentaries on the stotram exist from Shringeri and Kanchi mathadhipatis, the source attribution to Adi Shankara himself is consistent across every published recension on Sanskrit Documents, Stotra Nidhi and ReSanskrit.

seoFaqs.question: How many verses does the Ardhanareeshwara Stotram contain?
seoFaqs.answer: The stotram has eight (ashta) main verses praising the joint form of Shiva-Parvati, followed by a ninth phalashruti verse that declares the spiritual fruit of recitation. This 8+1 structure is a Shankara hallmark seen also in his Annapurna and Kalabhairava stotrams. Each of the eight body verses ends with the refrain 'ardhanareeshwaram' (or 'ardhanarishwaram devam'), creating a memorable rhythmic anchor. The opening verse 'Champeya gowrardha shareerakaayai' begins by describing one half as champaka-flower-golden (Parvati) and the other half as smeared with sacred ash (Shiva), a poetic device that recurs throughout.

seoFaqs.question: What is the spiritual meaning of the Ardhanareeshwara form praised in this stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ardhanareeshwara icon depicts Shiva (purusha, consciousness) and Parvati (prakriti, energy) fused into a single body split vertically down the middle. Adi Shankara's stotram emphasises that this is not merely an artistic motif but a non-dualistic statement: liberating insight (jnana) and creative power (shakti) are the same reality viewed from two angles. Iconographic markers explicitly named in the verses include Parvati's side carrying the lotus, gold ornaments, and the silken garment, while Shiva's side carries the trishula, serpent, tiger skin, and matted locks with Ganga. The hymn is therefore widely cited in Shaiva-Shakta dialogue as a textual proof of the equality of the two principles.

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Ardhanareeshwara Stotram phalashruti promise?
seoFaqs.answer: The concluding ninth verse states that one who recites these eight verses with devotion attains 'kirti, ayur, sampat, sarva mangala' (fame, long life, wealth and every auspicious thing) for eternity. Beyond material benefits, traditional commentators including the Shringeri matha highlight three deeper outcomes: harmony in domestic life (because the deity itself models conjugal union), dissolution of binary thinking that obstructs Advaita realisation, and protection from premature widowhood for married women. For these reasons it is the canonical stotram chanted by couples on Pradosham, Maha Shivaratri, and on the Kalyanotsavam (divine wedding) days at South Indian Shiva temples.

seoFaqs.question: When is Ardhanareeshwara Stotram traditionally recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The hymn is most often chanted on Pradosham (the 13th lunar day, twice a month), Maha Shivaratri, Mondays (Somavara) sacred to Shiva, and Fridays sacred to the Goddess. South Indian Shiva temples include it in the Kalyanotsavam (celestial-wedding) liturgy where the utsava-murti of Shiva and Parvati are seated together. Newly married couples often recite it on Pournami evenings during the first year of marriage as part of mangalya-prarthana. Because the stotram is short (only nine verses), it is also commonly inserted between the Lingashtakam and Bilvashtakam during the abhishekam puja at Tiruvannamalai, Chidambaram, and Madurai Meenakshi temples.

seoFaqs.question: Does the Ardhanareeshwara Stotram require initiation to chant?
seoFaqs.answer: No. As a Shankara-composed stotra it falls outside the diksha-restricted category of Tantric mantras and is openly recommended for all devotees, including children learning Sanskrit recitation. The only adhikara expectation is reverence and reasonably accurate pronunciation of compound words such as 'champeyagaurardhasharirakayai.' Many Shaiva matha schools, including the Sringeri Sadhana Sapta and Kanchi Kamakoti Veda Patashalas, teach this stotram in the second or third year of stotra-pathasala curriculum precisely because it trains students in dual-gender Sanskrit declensions while remaining doctrinally accessible.
